normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem lca_bst_subtree_witness (t : Tree) (p q a : Int)
  (hbst : IsBst t) (hp : HasKey t p) (hq : HasKey t q)
  (hr : lcaBst t p q = some a) :
  ∃ sub : Tree,
    IsSubtree sub t ∧
    (match sub with
     | Tree.nil => False
     | Tree.node _ k _ => k = a) ∧
    HasKey sub p ∧
    HasKey sub q := by
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: When the input is a BST containing both query keys, the returned key is the root of a subtree containing both queries.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem lca_bst_no_proper_descendant_both_keys (t : Tree) (p q a : Int)
  (hbst : IsBst t) (hp : HasKey t p) (hq : HasKey t q)
  (hr : lcaBst t p q = some a) :
  ∀ sub : Tree,
    IsProperSubtree sub (subtreeRootedAt t a) →
    ¬ (HasKey sub p ∧ HasKey sub q) := by
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: No proper subtree strictly below the returned LCA root contains both query keys (lowestness in proper-descendant form).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem lca_bst_self_key (t : Tree) (p : Int)
  (hbst : IsBst t) (hp : HasKey t p) :
  lcaBst t p p = some p := by
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: When the input is a BST containing the key, the LCA of a key with itself is that key.
